perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to during the term of this Agreement and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the Recipient exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a r easonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to during the term of this Agreement and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as confidential. The level of care in the protection of the Confidential Information will be determined on a case-by-case basis at the discretion of the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a conflict by altering the standard of care required for protecting Confidential Information. The original text mandates a level of care equivalent to that used for the Recipient's own proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree. The changed text grants the Recipient discretion to determine the level of care, which contradicts the original's defined minimum standard. This change directly contradicts section 9 which states NASDAQ OMX would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that NASDAQ OMX, b ecause of the nature of the Confidential Information, would suffer irreparable harm in the even t of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate NASDAQ OMX for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened material breach by the Recipient of any such provisions, NASDAQ OMX shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to inju nctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continua tion of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by NASDAQ OMX. If NASDAQ OMX prevails in any action brought to enjoin a material breach or threatened breach of this Confidentiality Agreement, it shall be entitled to reasonable attorney's fees and costs in connection with such legal proceeding. This Confidentiality Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its principles of conflicts of laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a minimum standard of care for Confidential Information, while the changed text grants the Recipient discretion to determine the level of care on a case-by-case basis. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that NASDAQ OMX would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach of the confidentiality agreement, as the altered standard of care introduces uncertainty and could allow for inadequate protection of confidential information, leading to such a breach.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential In formation to any person or entity, except its employees or partners involved in the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Recipient acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by Recipient and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with Recipient may also constitute a violation of the Inside r Trading Act and/or other securities and other laws. Access to or disclosure of Confidential Information by Recipient shall be limited to those employees or partners covered by a then effective legal obligati on or agreement with Reci pient that prohibits the disclosure or unauthorized use of confidential in formation deemed confidential by Recipient ( Authorized Employees ). The existence of such an agreement with an employee or partner of the Recipient shall not operate to relieve the Recipient fro m liability for acts of such employee or partner that result in a breach of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential In formation to any person or entity, except its employees or partners involved in the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Recipient acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by Recipient and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with Recipient may also constitute a violation of the Inside r Trading Act and/or other securities and other laws. Access to or disclosure of Confidential Information by Recipient shall be limited to any individual or entity whom the Recipient deems necessary for project completion. The existence of a non-disclosure agreement with such individual or entity is optional.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by loosening the restrictions on who can access Confidential Information. Previously, access was limited to Authorized Employees covered by a confidentiality agreement. Now, access extends to anyone the Recipient deems necessary, and a non-disclosure agreement is optional. This contradicts the original intent of strict confidentiality and increases the risk of unauthorized disclosure. The original text mentions there could be other securities laws that would be broken, and this would contradict that thought.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential In formation to any person or entity, except its employees or partners involved in the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Recipient acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by Recipient and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with Recipient may also constitute a violation of the Inside r Trading Act and/or other securities and other laws. Access to or disclosure of Confidential Information by Recipient shall be limited to any individual or entity whom the Recipient deems necessary for project completion. The existence of a non-disclosure agreement with such individual or entity is optional.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text and contradicted text limit access to confidential information to 'Authorized Employees' covered by a confidentiality agreement. The changed text broadens this to 'any individual or entity whom the Recipient deems necessary for project completion' and makes a non-disclosure agreement optional. This directly contradicts the original requirement for strict confidentiality and creates a conflict with the intention of compliance with other securities laws, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Notes and other documents refe rencing or relating to Confidentia l Information may be made and kept by the Recipient, but shall continue to be gove rned by this Confidentiality Agreement until they are destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Notes and other documents refe rencing or relating to Confidentia l Information may be made and kept by the Recipient, and the Recipient may choose to either destroy them or retain them indefinitely, regardless of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the handling of notes referencing Confidential Information. Initially, such notes remained governed by the Confidentiality Agreement until destruction. Now, the Recipient has the option to retain them indefinitely, irrespective of the agreement. This undermines the ongoing confidentiality obligations and is in contradiction with section 10 which states the confidentality agreement should be valid for 3 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The representations and obligations of the Reci pient relative to the Confidential Information shall survive the termination of this Confidentiality Agreem ent for a period up to three (3) years from the date of the completion or other termination of the Project.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows the Recipient to retain confidential information indefinitely, regardless of the Confidentiality Agreement. The contradicted text states the confidentiality obligations survive termination of the agreement for three years. This creates a direct conflict: one section allows indefinite retention without confidentiality, while the other mandates confidentiality for three years post-termination.

perturbation.original_text: All intellectual property rights associated with the Confidential Information, including without limitation, patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret rights, and moral rights shall remain in NASDAQ OMX.
perturbation.changed_text: All intellectual property rights associated with the Confidential Information, including without limitation, patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret rights, and moral rights may be jointly owned by NASDAQ OMX and the Recipient, as determined by a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original assignment of all intellectual property rights to NASDAQ OMX. By introducing the possibility of joint ownership based on a separate agreement, it creates uncertainty and conflicts with the initial unambiguous statement. The text previously was certain that all intellectual property rights remain in NASDAQ OMX, and this clause is now being modified to include the Recipient. This contradicts section 11 which contains that the complete understanding is between both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality Agreement contains the full and complete understanding of Recipient and NASDAQ OMX with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior representations and understandings whether they be oral or written.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all intellectual property rights remain in NASDAQ OMX. The changed text introduces the possibility of joint ownership with the Recipient, creating a direct conflict. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Confidentiality Agreement contains the full and complete understanding between both parties, as the changed text suggests a separate agreement may dictate ownership, which is a direct contradiction, according to the prompt's explanation.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Recipient receives a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Information, Recipient shall provide prompt actual notice to the General Counsel of NASDAQ OMX of such receipt, providing the Corporations with a reasonable opportunity to intervene in the proceeding before the time that Recipient is required to comply with such subpoena or other process. In the event that NASDAQ OMX instructs Recipient not to comply with any portion or all of the process, Recipient shall comply with such instructions and NASDAQ OMX shall indemnify and hold harmless the Recipient from any damages to th e extent that Recipient's damages result from its compliance with NASDAQ OMX's instructions.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Recipient receives a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Information, Recipient is solely responsible for determining the appropriate response and is not obligated to notify NASDAQ OMX. Recipient is responsible for all costs and damages.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original procedure for handling subpoenas. Originally, the Recipient was required to notify NASDAQ OMX and follow their instructions, with NASDAQ OMX providing indemnification. Now, the Recipient is solely responsible and bears all costs, creating a stark conflict in obligations and responsibilities. This change would directly go against section 9, in the scenario that NASDAQ OMX would suffer irreparable harm.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that NASDAQ OMX, b ecause of the nature of the Confidential Information, would suffer irreparable harm in the even t of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate NASDAQ OMX for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened material breach by the Recipient of any such provisions, NASDAQ OMX shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to inju nctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continua tion of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by NASDAQ OMX. If NASDAQ OMX prevails in any action brought to enjoin a material breach or threatened breach of this Confidentiality Agreement, it shall be entitled to reasonable attorney's fees and costs in connection with such legal proceeding. This Confidentiality Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its principles of conflicts of laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the responsibility for handling subpoenas entirely to the Recipient, removing the obligation to notify NASDAQ OMX and follow their instructions, which directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that NASDAQ OMX would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach, such as improper handling of confidential information disclosed via subpoena. By removing NASDAQ OMX's control and indemnification obligations, the changed text creates a situation where a breach is more likely, and NASDAQ OMX's ability to seek injunctive relief is compromised, hence leading to a direct contradiction.
